home.title: Verónica Velázquez
home.bio: Frontend Engineer specialized in building scalable and maintainable web applications. Strong experience within the React and TypeScript ecosystem, actively contributing to frontend architecture decisions and application state design in product-oriented environments. Focused on clean code, performance optimization, and technical solutions designed for sustainable growth.
home.cover_letter: I am a Frontend Engineer specialized in building scalable and maintainable applications using React and TypeScript. Over the past few years, I have worked on designing and evolving frontend architectures, managing complex global state with Redux, and delivering robust solutions in product-oriented environments. In my most recent role at MonoM (2022–2025), I led technical decisions related to frontend architecture, application state design, and reusable component patterns, with a strong focus on performance, long-term maintainability, and technical debt reduction. My background in UX/UI design allows me to approach frontend development not only from an engineering perspective, but also with a clear understanding of user experience and product coherence.

tasks: Active contribution to frontend architecture decisions using React and TypeScript.
Designed scalable global state management systems using Redux.
Implemented schema-based dynamic forms using react-jsonschema-form.
Developed advanced filtering systems and complex UI interactions with @dnd-kit.
Managed authentication and session flows using Firebase.
Defined reusable component patterns and frontend best practices.
Reduced technical debt and improved performance through strategic refactoring.
Developed micro-frontend architecture projects using React.
Worked in Agile environments collaborating with cross-functional teams.
Functional analysis, feature development, and incident resolution.
